APIs for managing workflow executions
Starts a new workflow execution based on a registered workflow definition.
Retrieves the current status and details of a running or completed workflow execution.

query.description: Whether to include task details in the response

response.name: Successful operation

description.content: Removes a workflow execution and its associated data.

query.description: Whether to archive the workflow before deletion

description.content: Pauses a currently running workflow execution.
Resumes a previously paused workflow execution.
Restarts a completed workflow from the beginning with the same input. If useLatestDefinitions is set, the workflow uses the latest definition.

query.description: Whether to use the latest workflow and task definitions

description.content: Retries the last failed task in a workflow execution.
Terminates a running workflow execution with an optional reason.

item.name: Conductor Skip a Task in a Running Workflow

request.name: Conductor Skip a Task in a Running Workflow

description.content: Skips a given task from a running workflow and moves to the next task. The skipped task will be marked as completed with the provided output.

variable.description: (Required) The reference name of the task to skip
(Required) The reference name of the task to skip
(Required) The reference name of the task to skip

description.content: Searches for workflow executions using Conductor's query language. Uses Elasticsearch for indexing workflow execution data.

query.description: Free text search across workflow data

description.content: Retrieves a list of running workflow instance IDs for a given workflow name and optionally filtered by version.

Conductor is a workflow orchestration platform originally created by Netflix. It allows you to build complex applications using simple and granular tasks that do not need to be aware of or keep track of the state of your application's execution flow. Conductor keeps track of the state, calls tasks in the right order (sequentially or in parallel, as defined by you), retries calls if needed, handles failure scenarios gracefully, and outputs the final result. This API provides endpoints for managing workflow definitions, task definitions, workflow executions, task executions, and event handlers.

Contact Support:
 Name: Conductor OSS
